data.title: JPMorgan Active High Yield ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.2622
data.description:
• JPMorgan Active High Yield ETF (JPHY) - $0.2622.
  • Payable May 05; for shareholders of record May 01; ex-div May 01.

data.title: Carter’s names Sharon Price John as CEO, replacing Douglas Palladini
data.description:
• Carter's Inc (CRI) announced that Sharon Price John has been appointed as the chief executive officer and president, effective June 15, 2026, and will join the board.
  • Richard F. Westenberger will serve as interim CEO and president alongside his CFO/COO role until the transition.
  • John joins from Build-A-Bear Workshop, where she led 13 years of growth, profitability improvement, and business transformation.
  • Her prior experience includes leadership roles at Stride Rite, Hasbro, and Mattel. Douglas C. Palladini has departed as CEO, President, and board member effective immediately.
  • The company reaffirmed its Q1 and full-year fiscal 2026 outlook and will report earnings on May 6, 2026.

data.title: Owens Corning appoints Todd Fister as chief financial and operating officer
data.description:
• Owens Corning (OC) announced on Friday the promotion of Todd Fister to executive vice president and chief financial and operating officer, effective May 1, 2026.
  • The company said the expanded role will combine operational and financial leadership responsibilities while Owens Corning conducts an external search for a new chief financial officer.
  • The appointment follows the company’s recent sale of its glass reinforcements business and is presented by management as part of a shift toward operating as a more integrated, focused building products company serving primarily residential markets in North America and Europe.
  • In his combined role, Fister will be responsible for executing enterprise initiatives intended to accelerate organic growth, enhance margins, and strengthen market positions through application of what the company calls The OC Advantages: its brand, commercial capabilities, technology, and cost position.
  • Fister joined Owens Corning in 2014 and previously served as executive vice president and chief financial officer since 2023. He was president of the company’s insulation business from 2019 to 2023 and was cited in the release as a key architect of the enterprise strategy positioning Owens Corning for organic growth and market-leading margins. Before Owens Corning, Fister held leadership roles in finance, corporate development, and strategy at MeadWestvaco (now part of Smurfit Kappa), Kimberly-Clark, and Procter & Gamble.

data.title: Atlassian surges after 'solid' Q3 results see praise from analysts
data.description:
Shares of Atlassian (TEAM) surged about 19% premarket on Friday after third-quarter results and outlook, which beat estimates, drew positive reactions from analysts.

Morgan Stanley kept its Overweight rating and $120 price target on the stock.

"A solid Q3 print highlighted by accelerating Cloud revenues beating consensus expectations by 4.5%, strong operating margins driving non-GAAP EPS >30% above consensus, and 4Q targets for Cloud and Operating Income both exceeding expectations should yield a solid relief rally against very weak investor sentiment (TEAM came into the print down almost 60% YTD)," said analysts led by Keith Weiss.

The analysts noted that next week's Analyst Day gives the management team the opportunity to further enhance their disclosures to give investors — more visibility into the underlying demand trends in Data Center, which get obfuscated by recent accounting changes, potentially with disclosure of annual recurring revenue, or ARR; and more confidence in the durability of the growth drivers powering the acceleration in Cloud revenues seen in the third quarter.

"Bottom-line, given a stock trading at 10X EV/CY27e FCF (at after hours price levels) for a company proving out the durability of 20%+ top-line growth and sustained operating leverage, we see significantly more runway for share appreciation and remain firmly OW," said Weiss and his team.

data.title: Western Digital's gross margin guidance more modest than some analysts expected
data.description:
Western Digital's (WDC) third-quarter revenue and outlook easily surpassed market estimates, but some analysts pointed to a more modest gross margin increase for the fourth quarter than anticipated.

Shares had declined 8% during early trading on Friday. However, shares have surged more than 150% year-to-date, and analysts maintain high ratings and price targets on the stock as the company continues to take advantage of the AI-driven supercycle.

"While sales gains look to remain solid (with revenues up $313M at the midpoint), GMs are only expected to grow by 100 bps (at the midpoint)," said Wedbush analysts Matt Bryson and Antoine Legault in a Friday note. "Net even while the guide was well ahead of prior numbers, the slower GM expansion raises some questions about WD's ability to continue to raise pricing (at least in the near-term)."

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating and $530 price target on Western Digital.

Western Digital's 50.5% adjusted gross margin for the third quarter marked the first time the company surpassed the 50% threshold. The company guided for a fourth-quarter gross margin ranging from 51% to 52%.

"Drivers include continued mix shift to higher capacity drives, UltraSMR adoption, and an ongoing favorable pricing environment," Bryson noted. "Given WDC's consistent tendency to guide conservatively, we would not be surprised if results again exceed the top end of the range."

Meanwhile, J.P. Morgan reiterated its Overweight rating and increased its price target to $530 from $400.

"Looking beyond the near term, the structural demand narrative continues to strengthen as confluence of inference workloads (expected to account for roughly two-thirds of all AI compute in 2026 as per management); Agentic AI applications which continuously execute workflows and generate persistent data; and Physical AI applications which create massive synthetic datasets — all together should collectively support a compounding loop of data generation and long-term exabyte demand growth well ahead of company's exabyte ramp guidance for mid-20% CAGR evidenced in extended visibility through LTAs extending into CY28 and CY29," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Samik Chatterjee, in a Friday note.

Western Digital's primary competitor, Seagate Technology (STX), was down about 2% during early Friday trading.

data.title: Rémy Cointreau SA reports FY results
data.description:
• Rémy Cointreau SA press release (REMYF): FY Revenue of €935.3M.
  • Liqueurs & Spirits (-0.1% on an organic basis in Q4):
    • Good momentum in both the US and China
    • More mixed performances in the EMEA4 region, in a muted consumption environment
  • 2025-26 organic COP target confirmed: organic decline in the low double-digit to mid-teens range

data.title: Iran sends Pakistan its latest proposal for U.S. talks - report
data.description:
Iran has passed its most recent written proposal for talks with the U.S. to Pakistan, which is acting as a mediator, according to a recent report from the state-run Islamic Republic News Agency.

The report did not disclose the details of the proposal.

Meanwhile, oil (USO) (BNO) and gas (UNG) prices remain elevated amid worries over a prolonged conflict while just a trickle of oil gets through the Strait of Hormuz. 

The U.S. and Iran continue to lock horns in a prolonged standoff, with President Donald Trump reaffirming his commitment to a naval blockade of Iranian ports despite surging oil prices and global economic concerns. The U.S. Central Command has reportedly requested to deploy the Dark Eagle long-range hypersonic missile to the Gulf region for possible use against Iran.

data.title: Esperion jumps on $1.1 billion ARCHIMED buyout
data.description:
• Esperion Therapeutics (NASDAQ: ESPR) shares jumped about 57% in premarket trading on Friday after the drugmaker agreed to be acquired by healthcare investment firm ARCHIMED in a deal valued at up to $1.1 billion.
  • Under the terms, shareholders will receive $3.16 per share in cash plus contingent value rights tied to future sales milestones worth up to $100 million, representing a 58% premium to the stock’s last close.
  • The transaction, which has been approved by Esperion’s board, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, after which the company will go private.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Parex Resources Inc. GAAP EPS of $0.05
data.description:
• Parex Resources Inc. press release (PXT:CA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.05.
  • FFO of $1.18
  • To recognize adjusted EBITDA(6) of $133 million, compared to $135 million in Q1 2025.
  • To incur $91 million of capital expenditures(6), compared to $57 million in Q1 2025. The increase from the prior year is primarily related to the ramping up of activity at the Orito and Occidente blocks in the Putumayo, and LLA-32 and LLA-111 in the Llanos, partially offset by lower activity at Cabrestero and LLA-74 in the Llanos.
  • Bank debt of $175 million as at March 31, 2026, compared to $50 million as at March 31, 2025. The increase was primarily due to the public acquisition of 6.1 million GeoPark Limited common shares and a $75 million deposit related to the Frontera Transaction.

data.title: CoreWeave rises as Citi ups price target
data.description:
• CoreWeave (CRWV) shares rose 2.5% in premarket trading as Citi upped its price target on the cloud computing company, citing increased demand and spending.
  • “The stars continue to align for AI infrastructure leaders like CRWV, and we estimate at least +35-40% QoQ backlog growth this Q as diversification across hyperscalers, AI labs, and enterprise de-risks customer concentration,” analyst Tyler Radke wrote in a note to clients. “The new Jane Street+META deal should likely ramp through end-FY27 and have potential to push CRWV above its $30B ARR target, with Anthropic as a call option. Near-term, rising Blackwell pricing may allow for some revenue upside as CRWV has potentially held onto a small amount of FY26 CapEx. With new IG debt lowering capital costs, EPS revisions could trend higher through FY26.” 
  • Radke reiterated his Buy rating on CoreWeave and raised his price target to $155 from $126.
